key_points: Force is an interaction that can change the motion, direction, or shape of an object
When multiple forces act on an object, the overall effect depends on the net force acting on it
Balanced forces result in no change in the state of motion, while unbalanced forces cause acceleration
Friction arises due to irregularities on surfaces in contact and always opposes motion
Non-contact forces such as gravitational, magnetic, and electrostatic forces act without physical contact
examples: Two teams pulling a rope with equal strength in a tug-of-war create balanced forces
A car accelerates when the engine produces a force greater than friction and air resistance
A magnet attracting iron filings demonstrates magnetic force
A charged comb attracting small paper pieces shows electrostatic force
formulas: Net force = Sum of all forces acting on an object
Unbalanced forces → Net force causes change in motion
exam_tips: Clearly distinguish between contact and non-contact forces in answers
Explain friction using the concept of surface irregularities
Always mention net force when discussing multiple forces acting on an object
Use real-life examples such as vehicles, sports, and magnets to explain concepts
